content: I don't know the best way to rewrite it

yeah there's a very strong implicit assumption here that wod/covenant count as part of the awakener but that's not a very natural assumption to make for newer players
equipment is honestly probably the best way to go about it
simply ask the devs to include what equipment is in the tutorial =^)

average Tawil moment, breaking the game as usual
yeah which is what made me rethink the idea of a body part
I like "order" or "command" like is currently used in game
since that's pretty much what is happening
Command can absolutely not be used in this context
the thing is the tentacles ARE basically some mystical fuckery that you're ordering to attack
command seems fine to me
it's the same as compel
you're commanding the tentacles to do something
But it's not a Command though
not sure i follow
they are, although whether they're just Tulu's subconcious or some other entities I'm unsure
telling the tentacles to attack = that's a command
is it an issue with "command" cards
okay see I feel like that would be a wild reach
we can't just shy away from all uses of words in unrelated contexts just because they're used once somewhere else in the game
nobody is going to confuse command cards with tentacles
sac trigger after remove coccon
so it diff with poison and bleed ?
Still best to avoid using very specific terminology where it's not relevant

I do agree with this, but if the devs keyword it it's fine
and apparently the devs ARE keywording things in the future so I think flavor renames are more applicable now
if compel or command is an issue you could use 'make'?
this was linked earlier for reference
I think we could just use "Order" at that point
I think "Make" makes the required sentence to wordy
or sounds a bit soft
...why not use Trigger?
i think trigger's the best out of the options
Trigger is fine
Trigger 1 Tentacle to attack sounds kinda funny to me though
vs Order 1 Tentacle to attack
would it be Trigger 1 Tentacle to attack or Trigger 1 Tentacle attack
in the case of aurita rouse, i think trigger is the best

there wouldn't be a "to"
Trigger 1 Tentacle attack.
the idea of aurita being mindlinked to a higher order entity in order to command it to attack in sync with her flinging jellyfish at an enemy is hilarious
don't mess with her friends
but I'm writing up some poll options and I'll put it in Mythag University server in a minute
wouldn't it be funny if you rotated this like 2° every day until people notice
see this was my thought because "Tentacle attack" is in essence a keyword here since it's referring to a specific mechanic but now I'm wondering how to word something like Tulu 4cost
Trigger 1 Tentacle attack for each Tentacle, i guess
but that sounds so redundant
why would you SAY that
because it's funny

Aftershock: Trigger 1 Tentacle attack.

or something like that
oh
i like how it switches between make and command on the same card
wait i mixed it up
yeah tulu base cost4 triggers a full tentacle salvoy

Easy?
> Deal X Pierce DMG to all enemies. (DMG benefits from 36% of Tentacle DMG.)
> Trigger all Tentacles to attack each enemy, dealing Pierce DMG.
tulu e1 makes the full tentacle salvoy aoe
we use "to" there because we put the plural s in Tentacles
well while we're on this topic do we want to consider renames for the other realm mechanics?
something about it just doesn't naturally read as aoe to me even though grammatically there's no issue, not sure why
well let's try the other styles
Trigger all Tentacles to attack once, dealing Pierce DMG to all enemies with each hit?
that's so wordy

Trigger all Tentacles to attack each enemy, dealing Pierce DMG.
vs
Order all Tentacles to attack each enemy, dealing Pierce DMG.
vs
Command all Tentacles to attack each enemy, dealing Pierce DMG.
I think it's the specification of each enemy vs all enemies

attack all enemies
as opposed to
attack each enemy

You're right, I would actually swap all and each there
> Trigger each Tentacle to attack all enemies.

insane sentence
tulu goliath in a team w a decently invested aliemus regen build hits for like 6 digit damage on abyss order
for every point of Aliemus Regen Level that Tulu has outside of combat....?
well no because then it reads like it's calling out of combat but inmap stats, which would include on exploration start mechanics but don't contribute towards passives
I've just been culling that <:karen_nervous:1408324291237511259> but I now realize it's not obvious that WoD & Covenant are part of the Awakener's effects
yeah I get that
> For every point of Aliemus Regen Level Tulu and his equipment have,
any thoughts on "Generate/obtain/gain X Tentacle" -> "Spawn X Tentacle" or is "Spawn" already used elsewhere/we don't like "Spawn"
because it's not consistent in game atm
Tulu has Gain, Sanga has Obtain, GMurphy has Generate
spawn does seem like the most fitting verb for tentacles to me
it's flavor vs practicality here
not a big deal if we go for something basic
but at the very least it should be consistent
spawn gives me a sort've 'making a summon/npc' vibe. not inaccurate but for a new player 'gain' might be clearer

I've been using "gain" because "summon" just doesn't sound right
But spawn is now my preference
I'll put up a poll for it
well tentacles are unique enough of a mechanic that I don't think there's much potential for confusion there
you could honestly use just about anything and i don't think it'd be possible to misinterpret the effect of getting more tentacles
"materialize" a tentacle
I also opted for the generic "trigger `N` Tentacle attack(s)" because I was told "compel a Tentacle to attack" was unliked but I'm gonna put that up into a poll as well while I'm at it
yeah compel is too unusual for me
i mostly see it in like, DnD/summoner based style of text
Fits so well for Tulu tho
which, arguably tentacles are a summon
But yeah
but tentacles aren't a separate sentient construct
it'd be like saying compel your arm to attack, it sounds odd because you're controlling your arm already so there isn't a second conscious party to compel
Fair
though see now this raises lore questions, because my assumption has implicitly been that tentacles are basically a body part a la tulu
but are they....
they're teamwide so are the tentacles actually separate beings linked to, say, some eldritch entity that's being controlled not by them but just being summoned by the party
(these are not realistically relevant questions but I can't help but think)

Well not all Aequor Awakeners have tentacles themselves so diegetically it's not that

I've been under the assumption that each Tentacle is a pseudo-controllable entity coming through a kind of D-Rift, the Tentacle "slots" in the UI are triangle after all
And the animation has them just kind of appear from under the enemy
And then retreat back under the ground
